title: chore(version): Fix failing tests when using algoliasearch@v3
body:
Partial reverse #4938 changes.

### Ts-ignore in Tests

 Tests were failing when in `algoliasearch@v3` environment. 
TypeScript errors are silent using ` @ts-ignore` instead of the previously ` @ts-expect-error connector uses strings`
linked to #4916 

I rolled back to the functions that enforced the types, removing the one introduced in the previous PR as it was throwing the following type error:

```
src/connectors/geo-search/connectGeoSearch.ts:188:55 - error TS2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number[][] | undefined'.

188               .setQueryParameter('insideBoundingBox', boundingBox)
                                                          ~~~~~~~~~~~

src/connectors/geo-search/connectGeoSearch.ts:395:11 - error TS2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number[][] | undefined'.

395           uiState.geoSearch.boundingBox
              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

###  Doc
Added documentation in the contributing guide on how to check types. @Haroenv if you prefer I can create an apart PR with this. If it is something you would like to keep of course. 

Added documentation in the README for typescript users. It can imagine this is something you may not want as it makes the README longer. Feel free to say so. I can also open an issue about it for users searching for this specific answer! 

### Testing script
I added a testing script as well as it was a hard work to try it out :') 

###  Question

I tried to reproduce an environment that would throw the error. 
It uses
- A vanilla typescript project using [vite.js](https://vitejs.dev/guide/#trying-vite-online)
-  `instantsearch.js` package build of this branch
- Algoliasearch v3 and @types/algoliasearch
- Typescript

I did not manage to throw the error :( Any idea how I could recreate the error in a working app?
